statement.p: Mylas maximum lung capacity is {7.2\ {\rm L}}\text{.} If her lungs are full and she exhales at a rate of {0.8\ {\textstyle\frac{\rm\mathstrut L}{\rm\mathstrut s}}}\text{,} write an inequality where the solution set is the possible times when she still has at least {0.4\ {\rm L}} of air left in her lungs. There is no need to solve it.
